CCN: 51E154 · HINTON, WV 25951 · Summers County

115 SUMMERS HOSPITAL ROAD, SUITE 300, HINTON, WV 25951

Setting

4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.01 3.57 4.34 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.32 0.62 0.35 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.41 0.79 1.52 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.28 2.15 2.47 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.73 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.80
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.30
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.11
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
40.0%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.26
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.92
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-08-21 9 9 0 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-11-09 5 4 1 20 1 20
Total weighted health score
56.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (22)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

22 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-01-14 2025-03-12
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. E 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-08-21 2024-10-25
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2022-11-09 2022-12-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-11-09 2022-12-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-11-09 2022-12-21
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2022-11-09 2022-12-21
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2021-08-11 2021-09-10
